Video.summary: The Okavango Delta, a huge emerald oasis is the lifeblood of this extraordinary natural Eden.
Video.title: Okavango: Water in the Desert

Video.summary: Namibia is an arid, rough land. However, it shows no sign of losing popularity with animals and people.
Video.title: Namibia: Sands of Time

Video.summary: The West Coast of Madagascar is remote and inhospitable but home to an astonishing array of animals.
Video.title: The Wild West Coast

Video.summary: The Northeast of Madagascar is a major trading center and one of the most species-rich regions in the world.
Video.title: The Forest of the Northeast

Video.summary: Madagascar is an island paradise in the Indian Ocean, home to fun-loving folk and a unique natural landscape.
Video.title: The Multifaceted Inland

Video.summary: The Black Forest captivates all who visit and has a reputation for its unique landscape and inhabitants.
Video.title: Black Forest

Video.summary: The Vosges are home to countless species, encompassing mountains, valleys, forests, and fields.
Video.title: Wilderness In the Heart of Europe

Image.alt: Wilderness In the Heart of Europe
Wilderness In the Heart of Europe
Wilderness In the Heart of Europe

Video.summary: The emblem of The Vosges is the stork. Once gone almost entirely, there are now 400 resident breeding pairs.
Video.title: In the Valley of the Storks

Image.alt: In the Valley of the Storks
In the Valley of the Storks
In the Valley of the Storks

Video.summary: The Maasai Mara is one of the most breathtaking animal habitats in the world.
Video.title: On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise

Image.alt: On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise
On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise
On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise

Video.summary: There are few places on earth that are closer to the stars than the high Andes.
Video.title: Andes

Video.summary: Patagonia seems to be primarily uninhabited by man, which makes this harsh, wild land a paradise for animals.
Video.title: Untamed Wilderness

Video.summary: The Pantanal, in the southwest of Brazil, is the largest marsh in the world.
Video.title: From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls

Image.alt: From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls
From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls
From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls

Video.summary: There is so much to discover in the Swiss Jura mountains. It's is a natural landscape with many facets.
Video.title: High Up in the Jura Mountains

Image.alt: High Up in the Jura Mountains
High Up in the Jura Mountains
High Up in the Jura Mountains

Video.summary: Ngorongoro is a land full of wildlife, from proud lions to vast herds of wildebeest and elephants.
Video.title: Ngorongoro: Born of Fire

Video.summary: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.
Video.title: The Jura Mountains

Video.summary: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.
Video.title: Ticino

Video.summary: Learn how the crocodile, shark, and whale evolved over millions of years to become modern masters of survival.
Video.title: Survival of the Fittest

Video.summary: No one runs the Race of Life faster than the big cats, winners in the contest for survival in the wild.
Video.title: The Big Cats

Video.summary: Learn the ways animals have adapted to survive (and thrive!) in the harsh climates of the world's deserts.
Video.title: The Great Desert Race

Video.summary: Somehow, some of the world's largest creatures prosper on the African Savannah with a strict vegetarian diet.
Video.title: Herbivores

Video.summary: Explore how animals win the Race of Life from the point of view of food, territory, social status, and mates.
Video.title: Natural Warriors

Video.summary: Nothing beats speed when it comes to survival in the wild. Learn how some animals are literally born to run.
Video.title: Run Like the Wind

Video.summary: The human imagination is limited when it comes to animal senses.
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Sight

Video.summary: On this episode we travel from the desert to the forest to learn how creatures never miss an auditory trick.
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Hearing

Video.summary: On this episode of Super Senses, we get in touch with the feelings of wild animals.
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Touch

Video.summary: On this episode we examine how things taste, and how vital scent is from the animal perspective.
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Smell and Taste

Video.summary: There are senses we share with most animals, but what about those senses that humans aren't equipped with?
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Super Human Senses

Video.summary: There’s a whole world of sensations that animals are aware of but humans are completely unable to detect.
Video.grandparentTitle: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
Video.title: Sixth Sense

Video.summary: Finland is a country of geographic and cultural transition; at the same time with a very distinct identity.
Video.title: Finland

Video.summary: Iceland is a volcanic island with a magnificent environment. Why did humans decide to colonize Iceland?
Video.title: Iceland

Video.summary: Sápmi becomes coastal along Norway, where it splinters off into thousands of islets.
Video.title: Lapland
